faqs.question: How long do microgreens take to grow?
faqs.answer: Typically 6–8 days from sowing to harvest.

faqs.question: Are these organic?
faqs.answer: These are non-GMO seeds suitable for microgreen cultivation.

faqs.question: Do I need sunlight?
faqs.answer: Indirect sunlight is sufficient after germination.

faqs.question: Can I grow these in soil?
faqs.answer: Yes, but cocopeat or microgreen trays are recommended for cleaner harvest.

faqs.question: Can beginners grow these?
faqs.answer: Yes. Microgreens are beginner-friendly and fast-growing.

faqs.question: What is the shelf life of seeds?
faqs.answer: Seeds should be stored in a cool, dry place in an airtight container.

defaults.flavorProfile: Chosen for clean, vigorous microgreen harvests with consistent flavour expression.
description: {baseName} seeds are selected for reliable germination, strong early growth, and dense tray coverage for microgreen production.
These seeds are a practical choice for home growers, cafes, and repeated sowing schedules where consistency matters as much as flavour.
growingNotes: Sow {baseName} densely in trays for even canopy growth and clean harvests.
Keep the medium evenly moist with good airflow to support uniform germination.
Expect harvest in {growingTimeNote}, depending on temperature, humidity, and light.
healthHighlights: Useful for salad, garnish, and bowl production
Works well across beginner and repeat growers

growingSteps.title: Prepare the tray:
growingSteps.description: Fill a shallow tray with cocopeat or another growing medium and level the surface evenly.

growingSteps.title: Sow the seeds:
growingSteps.description: Evenly sprinkle {baseName} seeds across the tray without overcrowding.

growingSteps.title: Mist gently:
growingSteps.description: Lightly mist the tray to keep the growing medium evenly moist.

growingSteps.title: Blackout phase:
growingSteps.description: Cover the tray and keep it in a dark place for 2-3 days to allow seeds to germinate.

growingSteps.title: Move to light:
growingSteps.description: Once sprouts appear, remove the cover and place the tray under indirect sunlight or grow lights.

growingSteps.title: Maintain moisture:
growingSteps.description: Lightly mist the tray once or twice daily to keep the medium moist.

growingSteps.title: Ensure airflow:
growingSteps.description: Keep the tray in a well-ventilated area to support healthy growth.

growingSteps.title: Harvest:
growingSteps.description: Harvest in {growingTime} when microgreens reach 2-3 inches in height.

infoSections.title: Flavor Profile
infoSections.description: {baseName} microgreens have a fresh, harvest-ready flavor profile suited to everyday use.

infoSections.title: Nutritional Highlights
infoSections.description: {baseName} microgreens offer a concentrated fresh harvest with useful wellness-supporting nutrients.

infoSections.title: Yield Information
infoSections.description: Yield depends on tray size, sowing density, moisture balance, and harvest timing.
items: Harvest at the right canopy stage

infoSections.title: Ideal Growing Conditions
items: Light: Indirect sunlight or grow lights

description: Radish Pink seeds grow into crisp microgreens with vibrant stems and a fresh peppery flavor. These fast-growing greens add taste and color to dishes such as sandwiches, salads, and chaat.
Radish microgreens grow very quickly and are ready to harvest in 6–8 days, making them one of the easiest microgreens to grow at home.
description1: Radish Pink microgreens are the young shoots of radish plants harvested early when their nutrients and natural flavors are concentrated. These microgreens produce colorful stems and crisp leaves with a bold peppery taste.
They are commonly used in sandwiches, chaat bowls, salads, and wraps, adding both visual appeal and flavor.
Radish microgreens are rich in vitamins, minerals, and antioxidants, supporting digestion and overall wellness.
At Microgreens de Manikonda, our radish seeds are selected to ensure high germination rates and healthy growth.
growingNotes: Sow seeds densely in trays for even canopy growth and clean harvests.
Keep the medium evenly moist with good airflow to support uniform germination.
Expect harvest in 5-8 days, depending on temperature, humidity, and light.
healthHighlights: High in vitamin C and antioxidants
Supports immune health and cellular protection

infoSections.title: Flavor Profile
infoSections.description: Radish Pink microgreens have a bold peppery flavor similar to radish roots.
infoSections.conclusion: They pair well with sandwiches, chaat, salads, and wraps.

infoSections.description: Radish Pink microgreens contain:
infoSections.conclusion: These nutrients support immune health and digestion.

infoSections.title: Yield Information
infoSections.description: Approximately 200 grams of seeds can produce 700-900 grams of fresh microgreens.

items: Light: Indirect sunlight or grow lights

description: Radish Purple Sango seeds grow into vibrant microgreens with deep purple stems and bright green leaves, offering a crisp texture and bold peppery flavor. These colorful microgreens add both taste and visual appeal to dishes such as sandwiches, salads, chaat, and grain bowls.
Radish Purple Sango microgreens grow quickly and are usually ready to harvest in 6–8 days, making them one of the fastest microgreens to grow at home.
description1: Radish Purple Sango microgreens are the young shoots of the radish plant harvested early when their nutrients and flavor compounds are highly concentrated. These microgreens develop striking purple stems with tender leaves that provide a fresh peppery taste.
Because of their vibrant appearance and crisp texture, they are commonly used in sandwiches, salads, wraps, and chaat bowls, adding both color and flavor to everyday meals.
Radish Purple Sango microgreens are rich in vitamins, antioxidants, and natural sulfur compounds, which help support digestion, detoxification, and immune health.
At Microgreens de Manikonda, our Radish Purple Sango seeds are carefully sourced to ensure strong germination and healthy growth for home growers and urban kitchens.
growingNotes: Sow seeds densely for uniform purple color development.
Maintain consistent moisture for even germination.
Harvest when true leaves appear for best color and flavor.
healthHighlights: Rich in anthocyanins and vitamin C

red-cabbage-microgreens-seeds.flavorProfile: Mild cabbage with slight peppery notes
description: Red cabbage seeds produce vibrant purple microgreens with a mild, slightly sweet cabbage flavor. These colorful greens are rich in antioxidants and vitamins and are commonly used in salads, wraps, grain bowls, and gourmet plating.
They grow quickly and are usually ready for harvest in 7–10 days, making them ideal for beginners.
description1: Red Cabbage microgreens are young shoots of the cabbage plant harvested early when their nutrients, flavor, and vibrant purple color are at their peak. These microgreens are known for their mild cabbage flavor, crisp stems, and tender leaves that add both nutrition and visual appeal to everyday meals.

Their mild taste makes them extremely versatile in the kitchen. Red Cabbage microgreens work well in salads, wraps, sandwiches, grain bowls, tacos, and fresh vegetable dishes, adding a subtle crunch and bright color to plates.
These microgreens are also valued for their high antioxidant content, particularly anthocyanins, which give them their deep purple color and support overall health.

At Microgreens de Manikonda, our Red Cabbage seeds are carefully sourced to ensure strong germination, consistent growth, and vibrant microgreens that are suitable for both beginner growers and experienced microgreens enthusiasts.
growingNotes: Sow seeds densely for rich color development.
Provide adequate light for vibrant purple coloration.
Maintain consistent moisture for uniform growth.
healthHighlights: Extremely high in vitamins C and K

infoSections.title: Flavor Profile
infoSections.description: Red cabbage microgreens have a mild cabbage flavor with slight sweetness.
infoSections.conclusion: They pair well with salads, wraps, tacos, sandwiches, and grain bowls.

infoSections.title: Nutritional Highlights
infoSections.description: Radish Pink microgreens contain:
infoSections.conclusion: These nutrients help support immune health, bone strength, cellular protection, and overall vitality.

infoSections.title: Yield Information
infoSections.description: Approximately 50 grams of Red Cabbage seeds can produce 300–400 grams of fresh microgreens, depending on tray density, watering practices, and growing conditions.

infoSections.title: Ideal Growing Conditions
items: Red Cabbage microgreens grow best in:
Light: Indirect sunlight or grow lights
Humidity: Moderate humidity with good air circulation
They are well suited for indoor growing, kitchen gardens, balcony setups, and small urban farming spaces.

red-carrot-microgreens-seeds.flavorProfile: Mild carrot flavor with slight sweetness
description: Red Carrot seeds grow into delicate microgreens with slender stems and feathery leaves, offering a mild carrot-like flavor with subtle sweetness. These microgreens add freshness and nutrition to dishes such as vegetable pulao, salads, sandwiches, and yogurt bowls.
Red Carrot microgreens grow steadily and are usually ready to harvest in 10–14 days, making them a great choice for home growers looking for flavorful and nutrient-rich greens.
description1: Red Carrot microgreens are the young shoots of carrot plants harvested early when their nutrients and antioxidants are highly concentrated. These microgreens develop delicate stems and finely textured leaves with a mild carrot flavor and subtle sweetness.
Because of their gentle taste and soft texture, red carrot microgreens blend well into vegetable pulao, raita, salads, sandwiches, and grain bowls, adding both flavor and nutritional value.
Red carrot microgreens are rich in beta-carotene, vitamins, and antioxidants, which help support vision, skin health, and immune function.
At Microgreens de Manikonda, our Red Carrot seeds are carefully selected for reliable germination and healthy growth, producing vibrant microgreens suitable for home cultivation.
growingNotes: Sow seeds sparingly for better air circulation.
Maintain even moisture for delicate seedlings.
Longer growing time requires patience and care.
healthHighlights: High in beta-carotene and vitamin A

description: Red Lentil (masoor) seeds grow into tender microgreens with soft leafy shoots and a mild earthy flavor. These nutrient-rich microgreens can be added to everyday dishes such as dal rice, vegetable upma, khichdi, salads, and wraps for extra plant-based nutrition.
Red Lentil (masoor) microgreens grow quickly and are usually ready to harvest in 8–10 days, making them an excellent option for home growers.
description1: Red Lentil microgreens are the young shoots of masoor dal plants harvested early when their nutrients and plant proteins are concentrated. These microgreens develop soft green leaves and tender stems with a mild earthy flavor.
Because masoor is a staple in Indian kitchens, these microgreens blend naturally into dal rice, khichdi, vegetable upma, salads, and wraps, adding both freshness and nutritional value.
Red lentil (masoor) microgreens are rich in plant-based protein, vitamins, and minerals, making them a healthy addition to vegetarian diets and balanced meals.
At Microgreens de Manikonda, our Red Lentil (masoor) seeds are selected for reliable germination and strong growth, producing healthy microgreens suitable for indoor growing.

sunflower-microgreens-seeds.flavorProfile: Nutty, slightly sweet with sunflower seed flavor
description: Sunflower seeds produce crunchy microgreens with thick stems and a pleasant nutty flavor. These microgreens are popular in salads, sandwiches, wraps, and healthy snack bowls.
Sunflower microgreens grow quickly and are typically ready to harvest in 7–10 days, making them one of the most popular microgreens for home growing.
description1: Sunflower microgreens are the young shoots of sunflower plants harvested early when their nutrients and flavor are most concentrated. These microgreens develop thick crunchy stems and broad green leaves with a mild nutty taste.
Their crunchy texture and pleasant flavor make them ideal for salads, sandwiches, wraps, grain bowls, and healthy snack bowls.
Sunflower microgreens are rich in plant protein, vitamins, and healthy fats, making them a nutritious addition to everyday meals.
At Microgreens de Manikonda, our Sunflower seeds are selected for high germination and strong growth, producing vibrant microgreens perfect for home cultivation.
growingNotes: Soak seeds for 4-8 hours before sowing.
Sow seeds densely for protein-rich harvests.
Maintain consistent moisture for optimal growth.

Rich in minerals and B vitamins

Swiss Chard microgreens grow quickly and are usually ready to harvest in 9–12 days, making them a great option for home growers looking for nutritious greens.

Swiss Chard microgreens are rich in vitamins, minerals, and antioxidants, which support overall health, immunity, and energy levels.

wheatgrass-microgreens-seeds.flavorProfile: Grassy, slightly sweet with earthy notes
description: Wheatgrass seeds grow into vibrant green shoots known for their fresh grassy flavor and powerful nutritional profile. Wheatgrass microgreens are commonly used in juices, detox drinks, smoothies, and wellness shots.
These microgreens grow quickly and are typically ready to harvest in 7–9 days, making them one of the most popular greens for home growing and daily nutrition.
description1: Wheatgrass microgreens are the young shoots of wheat plants harvested early when their nutrients and enzymes are highly concentrated. These slender green shoots have a refreshing grassy flavor and are widely used in detox juices, smoothies, and health drinks.
Because wheatgrass is known for its cleansing and energizing properties, it is commonly consumed as fresh wheatgrass juice or blended into smoothies and wellness shots.
Wheatgrass microgreens contain a wide range of vitamins, minerals, amino acids, and chlorophyll, making them popular for supporting overall health and vitality.
At Microgreens de Manikonda, our Wheatgrass seeds are selected for high germination and consistent growth, producing healthy microgreens ideal for indoor cultivation.
growingNotes: Sow seeds densely for nutritional density.
Maintain consistent moisture for optimal growth.
Provide adequate light for chlorophyll development.
healthHighlights: Extremely high in chlorophyll and vitamins

description: Yellow carrot seeds grow into delicate microgreens with soft feathery leaves and a mild carrot flavor. These microgreens add freshness and color to dishes like salads, sandwiches, vegetable bowls, and healthy wraps.
Yellow carrot microgreens grow slowly compared to many other microgreens and are typically ready to harvest in 12–16 days.
description1: Yellow carrot microgreens are the young shoots of carrot plants harvested early when their nutrients and natural plant compounds are concentrated. These microgreens develop thin stems and delicate feathery leaves with a subtle carrot flavor.
Because of their mild sweetness and fresh aroma, carrot microgreens work well in salads, sandwiches, wraps, and vegetable bowls, adding both flavor and visual appeal.
Carrot microgreens are rich in carotenoids, vitamins, and antioxidants, making them beneficial for eye health, immunity, and overall wellness.
At Microgreens de Manikonda, our Yellow Carrot seeds are selected for strong germination and healthy growth, producing vibrant microgreens suitable for indoor cultivation.
growingNotes: Sow seeds sparingly for better air circulation.
Maintain even moisture for delicate seedlings.
Longer growing time requires patience and care.
healthHighlights: High in beta-carotene and vitamin A

description: Alfalfa seeds produce delicate, mild-flavored microgreens that are rich in vitamins, minerals, and digestive enzymes. Known for their gentle nutty taste and tender texture, Alfalfa microgreens are perfect for salads, sandwiches, wraps, smoothies, and light meals.
These seeds are easy to grow and beginner-friendly, producing fresh microgreens in about 7–10 days, making them ideal for home growers and urban kitchens.
description1: Alfalfa microgreens seeds grow into tender young greens harvested early when their nutritional concentration is at its peak. These microgreens are known for their mild flavor, crisp texture, and subtle nutty taste that blends easily into everyday meals.

Because of their delicate flavor, Alfalfa microgreens are often used in salads, sandwiches, wraps, smoothies, and yogurt-based dishes, adding freshness and nutrition without overpowering other ingredients.
They are especially valued for their high mineral content and natural enzymes, which support digestion, metabolism, and overall wellness.

At Microgreens de Manikonda, our Alfalfa seeds are carefully sourced to ensure high germination rates, strong growth, and healthy microgreens, making them perfect for both beginners and experienced growers.
growingNotes: Soak seeds for 4-6 hours before sowing.
Sow seeds densely for nutritional density.
Maintain even moisture for optimal growth.
healthHighlights: Rich in vitamins K, C, and minerals
High in calcium, potassium, iron, and zinc
Supports immune function and bone health

amaranthus-red-garnet-microgreens-seeds.flavorProfile: Earthy, slightly sweet with beet-like notes
description: Amaranthus Red Garnet seeds grow into vibrant deep-red microgreens with a delicate texture and mild earthy flavor. These colorful greens are packed with essential vitamins, minerals, and antioxidants, making them a beautiful and nutritious addition to salads, grain bowls, and gourmet dishes.
These microgreens are moderately easy to grow and are typically ready to harvest in 8–12 days, making them a favorite among home growers and chefs.
description1: Amaranthus Red Garnet microgreens are known for their striking deep red color and delicate leaves. Harvested young when their nutrients are highly concentrated, these microgreens bring both visual appeal and nutritional value to a wide range of dishes.
Their mild earthy taste pairs particularly well with grain bowls, salads, roasted vegetables, and gourmet plating, making them popular in both home kitchens and restaurants.
Amaranthus microgreens are naturally rich in antioxidants, minerals, and plant compounds, helping support immune health and overall vitality.
At Microgreens de Manikonda, our Amaranthus Red Garnet seeds are carefully selected to ensure consistent germination, vibrant color development, and healthy microgreen growth.
growingNotes: Sow seeds densely for rich color development.
Maintain consistent moisture for optimal growth.
Provide adequate light for vibrant coloration.
healthHighlights: High in protein, iron, and vitamins A, C
